reasoning: To resolve positively, the number of 'Protests' in Myanmar from Feb 20, 2026 to Mar 21, 2026 must exceed 10 times the baseline (which is currently ~50 per month). This means we need >500 protests in 30 days. Peaceful protests in Myanmar largely stopped after the 2021 military crackdown, and the opposition movement has transformed into active armed resistance. Current ACLED reporting from early 2026 highlights intense armed conflict, rebel capture of outposts, and resistance attacks intended to disrupt junta-led elections. There is absolutely no reporting of a massive, peaceful protest wave (which is what the 'Protests' category strictly covers). Given the high threshold of >500 events and the complete absence of any evidence for a mass nonviolent movement in early 2026, the probability is at the floor.
